name: Coquilles St. Jacque
recipeInstructions:
1 pound sea scallops, whole - washed and dried
SAUCE
0.25 cup butter
0.25 cup onion - finely chopped
0.25 lb mushroom - sliced
0.25 cup flour, all-purpose
dash pepper
1 cup light cream
1 cup Gruyere cheese - grated
2 tablespoons dry white wine
0.5 cup court bouillon
0.5 tablespoon parsley - chopped
MASHED POTATOES
2 pounds white potatoes - peeled and chopped
0.25 cup butter
0.25 cup milk
salt and pepper
1 egg yolk
0.125 cup parmesan cheese
In a medium saucepan, combine ingredients for court bouillon and bring to a boil. Simmer 10 minutes and add 0.5 cup white wine. Add scallops, simmer 6 minutes, drain, and reserve liquid. Prepare the sauce by sautéing onions and mushrooms in half a cup of butter until tender. Remove from heat, stir in flour and pepper, then gradually stir in cream, bringing to a boil while stirring constantly. Reduce heat and simmer until thick (4-5 minutes). Add Gruyere cheese and stir until melted. Mix in white wine and the reserved court bouillon with parsley.
Add scallops into 6 scallop shells. Prepare mashed potatoes using an egg yolk. Pipe around the scallop shells with a pastry bag and star tip. Sprinkle with parmesan cheese. Place shells on a baking sheet and broil for 2-3 minutes until golden brown and heated through.
